title: Minimize the admission of Windows HostProcess Containers

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of Windows HostProcess Containers
input_row.description: Do not generally permit Windows containers to be run with the hostProcess flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A Windows container making use of the hostProcess flag can interact with the underlying Windows cluster node. As per the Kubernetes documentation, this provides "privileged access" to the Windows node. Where Windows containers are used inside a Kubernetes cluster, there should be at least one admission control policy which does not permit hostProcess Windows containers. If you need to run Windows containers which require hostProcess, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of hostProcess containers. This audit may also require that you review the securityContext in the Pod specification yaml.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostProcess containers. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostProcess containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/create-hostprocess-pod/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: Pods defined with securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_windows_hostprocess_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster using the Kubernetes API or `kubectl get namespaces`.
- For each namespace, check for the presence of Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels (e.g., `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce`) or PodSecurityPolicies (if still in use), or any other admission controller policies (such as OPA Gatekeeper, Kyverno) that restrict the use of Windows HostProcess containers.
- Specifically, verify that policies in each namespace deny the creation of pods with `spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess: true`.
- Use the following commands and API checks:
    - `kubectl get psp -o yaml` (if PodSecurityPolicy is enabled) and inspect for rules that deny or restrict `hostProcess: true`.
    - For Pod Security Admission, check namespace labels:
      ```
      kubectl get namespace --show-labels
      ```
      and ensure that the enforced policy is at least `baseline` or a custom policy that restricts `hostProcess: true`.
    - For OPA Gatekeeper/Kyverno, list all constraint templates and constraints, and inspect for policies that deny or restrict `hostProcess: true`.
    - Optionally, search for existing pods with:
      ```
      kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess == true)'
      ```
      to identify any non-compliant pods.
- Pass Criteria:
    - Every namespace with user workloads has an enforced policy (via PSA, PSP, or admission controller) that denies pods with `spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess: true`, except for namespaces explicitly allowed by exception policy.
    - No pods are running with `hostProcess: true` unless explicitly permitted by policy.
- Fail Criteria:
    - Any namespace lacks a policy restricting `hostProcess: true`, or pods are found running with `hostProcess: true` without explicit exception.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
